Juliani Hero Review - Empires and Puzzles

Juliani brings the terrifying Moonrise Strike to the 4-star arena, but his Slow speed clips his wings. Can this Owl find a perch in your Rush War defense, or is he destined to gather dust? We break down the math behind his unique damage mechanic and his poison-powered healing.
Juliani Review: The Owl Who's Just Too Slow to Give a Hoot?
Juliani: The Owl Tower's Budget Nuke (Batteries Not Included)

event. Released in early 2024, he brings the unique "Moonrise" damage mechanic—previously reserved for 5-star heavy hitters—down to the 4-star level. As a Wizard class hero, he has the potential to deal extra damage with Jinx, but his defining characteristic is his
mana speed. In a world of fast-paced battles, Juliani is the owl who slept in, woke up late, and decided to bring a Scroll of Spite to the party just to ruin everyone else's mood.

Juliani looks like he's seen things—terrible things. With an eyepatch that suggests a past squabble with a particularly aggressive squirrel and a "Scroll of Spite" that probably contains a list of everyone who ever wronged him (it's a long scroll), he fits the "grumpy old wizard" vibe perfectly. The art is detailed, featuring the signature Owl Tower purple and gold aesthetic, but let's be honest: he looks like the kind of owl who yells at owlets to get off his branch.
Juliani's stats were respectable upon his release in early 2024, sitting at
. However, looking at the landscape in 2026, he has been significantly power-crept.
Comparing him to a modern late-2025 4-star like

(Attack 794 / Defense 792 / Health 1502), Juliani is lagging behind by over 100 points in attack and defense, and over 200 in health. While his Limit Break 2 stats help close the gap, he is statistically an "old generation" 4-star. He relies heavily on his unique damage mechanics to stay relevant, as he can't win on raw stats alone anymore.

The "Moonrise Strike" is the star of the show. Unlike standard damage skills, this doesn't use a fixed percentage. Instead, it deals damage based on the difference between Juliani's Attack and the target's Defense.
860 damage. This is a hard cap. No matter how much you buff him, he won't hit for 2000 damage like his 5-star cousins.

The secondary effect is arguably more interesting for a 4-star. He deals fixed Poison damage and then heals his team for a percentage of that damage. This makes him a hybrid damage-dealer/healer, which is a rare and valuable role in 4-star tournaments.
The +30% damage against Holy targets means he can absolutely obliterate yellow heroes, making him a specific counter to heavy yellow defenses in tournaments.

If an enemy dies, the poison stops, and so does the healing from that enemy.
The -80% reduction in mana gained from Special Skills for enemies is a hidden gem. It essentially neuters heroes who rely on getting hit or passive mana generation to charge up.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities

The Owl family bonus is weird but potentially powerful. Replacing mana generation ailments (like those from Hel or Proteus) with a Defense buff is niche. It won't come up often in PvE, but in PvP against specific control heroes, it can turn a disadvantage into a tanky advantage.
Reduce Mana Gain from Special Skills (-80%):
This is massive. It prevents enemies from gaining mana when
use special skills (if they have mana-gain effects) or from things like being hit by special skills if they have specific passives. It effectively slows down the enemy team's momentum.
Simple and effective. Since his Moonrise Strike damage scales based on his Attack stat, this Aether Power directly contributes to him reaching his damage cap more easily.
When released, Juliani was seen as a "mini-Owl" who offered a taste of the new mechanics to F2P and C2P players. He was immediately identified as a Rush Attack specialist.
As noted in the Stats section, he has fallen behind. Modern 4-stars have stats that rival older 5-stars. Juliani's low defense makes him squishy for a Slow hero.
a general-purpose hero. You will not use him in standard raids or war attacks unless you are desperate. However, in
, he remains a top-tier threat. His slow speed becomes Very Fast, and his high damage cap + healing makes him a nightmare to deal with when the tiles are flying.
As of this review, Juliani does not have a costume.

The Moonrise Strike damage calculation depends on the difference between Juliani's Attack and the enemy's Defense. To consistently hit that 860 damage cap (especially against newer, high-def enemies), you need every point of Attack you can get.
He remains squishy and might die before firing.

. Juliani's only job is to fire once and devastate the enemy team. If he fires and does weak damage, he has failed. Give him the Attack power to ensure that one hit counts.

The Poison-to-Health conversion provides excellent sustain for him and his neighbors.

If his Attack is debuffed (Wither) or the enemy has high Defense (Growth), his damage suffers more than standard heroes.

You likely have faster, stronger options for general play. However, Juliani is a

. If you care about ranking high in 4-star Rush, he is worth the mats. Do not waste Alpha Aethers (LB2) on him unless you are overflowing with materials and really love the Owl aesthetic; his stats won't catch up to 2026 standards enough to matter outside his niche.

Juliani is a specialist, not a generalist. In the 4-star Rush arena, he is a kingpin, dropping devastating moon-hammers and drinking the poison of his enemies like a fine wine. Everywhere else, he's just a slow bird trying to catch a supersonic worm. Keep him for the specific weeks where speed is equalized, and he will serve you well. For daily driving? Leave him in the aviary.
